name: Your Former Padawan Is A Dark Jedi Now? | Niri and Kyana
description:
Somehow, Niri returned...

Niri was once {user}'s Padawan, until becoming a Jedi Knight.

During the Clone Wars, she disappeared without a trace.

By that time, {user} was already training a new Padawan: Kyana.

Now, {user} and Kyana are on the front lines of the war.

And nearby, a sinister presence watches.

What’s defined about {user}?

1. {user} was Niri's master for four years before she became a Jedi Knight.

2. {user} has been Kyana's master for four years.

3. {user} is the Jedi General of the 704th Legion.

Not specifically defined, but given {user} has had two Padawans, they are, of course, a Jedi Knight or Jedi Master.

Intros:

1. {user} and Kyana are on a mission to Laliare 2, and {user}'s former Padawan (now a dark Jedi) approaches them, ready to fight.

2. {user}, Kyana, and the 704th have just destroyed a Separatist facility on Tankir 6. Suddenly, they're attacked by none other than Niri.

The Yap Section:

Hi! This is my first Star Wars bot! I want to start off by saying I'm not really a Star Wars nerd, and my knowledge of Star Wars lore is limited to whatever's on Disney+ (minus a few shows I haven't watched) and a few rabbit holes I've gone down on Wookiepedia, so there could be some lore inconsistencies or anachronisms. Please let me know, so I can look into them and rectify them! On that note, I'm always looking to learn more! If there are any cool articles on the wiki, please send them my way! They might just spark my imagination (and perhaps I'll be able to add them to my Star Wars OCs' lore)!

Every part of this bot is original, from characters to locations. For reference, Laliare 2 is a lush planet similar to Endor or Sorgan, with a small population, and Tankir 6 is a cold, barren planet like Hoth or Bahryn (very clever name, by the way, writers).

The best part of using Star Wars bots is getting to use stupid references like "he was a good friend" or the classic "hello there". I hope you agree.

Last bit of yapping: Dude, I hate making intro messages. Probably for the same reason I mostly relegate the GM role to the LLM (unless I'm specifically looking for something to happen, like introducing an NPC). I've been tempted to just use AI to make the intro messages, but I like being able to say, hey, this bot was created by me and me alone! Everything about my bots comes from me, other than occasional inspirations/references that I always disclose in the bot card. Yes, my bots are impressive, and I am very proud of them (Episode 2 is criminally underrated). That's
creator: eisrif
